originalTitle: Four men held over child marriage in Sierra Leone appear in landmark court case
title: Good News for Girls in Sierra Leone: Every Child Deserves to Dream!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It's me, your friendly news reporter, bringing you a super important story from a beautiful country called Sierra Leone. It’s about how grown-ups are working hard to make sure every child, especially girls, can go to school and have big, happy dreams, just like you!

Big News for Girls

There's a special story happening right now in Sierra Leone. It's about a girl who is 17 years old. That means she's still a child, a teenager, just a few years older than some of you! When you are 17, you should be busy with school, playing with friends, and learning new things every day. You should be thinking about what you want to be when you grow up – maybe a doctor, a teacher, an engineer, or even a news reporter like me! But for this 17-year-old girl, things were not quite right. Some grown-ups wanted her to get married, even though she is still a child. This is not fair, and it's not allowed by the law in many places, including Sierra Leone now.

What is 'Child Marriage'?

Let's talk about something called 'child marriage'. It means when someone who is still a child – usually a girl – is made to marry an older person. This is like asking a little bird to fly before its wings are strong enough, or asking a tiny seed to grow into a big tree overnight. It's just not natural or right. Children need to be children. They need time to play, to learn, to grow, and to be cared for by their families. When a child is married, they often have to leave school. They can't play anymore. They have to do grown-up things that are too difficult for them. This makes them very sad and stops them from having a happy childhood. Did you know? Every child has rights! These are special rules that say what children need to grow up happy and healthy. One big right is the right to go to school!

Who Are the Grown-ups Involved?

In this story, there are four grown-up men who are in trouble because they were part of trying to make this 17-year-old girl get married. One of them is her own father. Another is the man they wanted her to marry. The other two also helped. It's very sad when grown-ups don't protect children, especially a father who should keep his child safe. Our laws are like special rules that everyone must follow to make sure our communities are fair and safe. These men are now in court because the law says what they did was wrong. They might have to go to jail for a long time if the court finds them guilty of breaking the law.

What is a 'Landmark Court Case'?

This is a very important story because it's called a 'landmark court case'. Imagine you are building a house, and you put down a very strong first stone. That stone helps build the whole house. A 'landmark court case' is like that strong first stone for new laws. It means this case is special because it will help make sure that other children are protected in the future. It sets an example for everyone. When people go to court, a judge listens to what happened. The judge tries to find out the truth and decides what is fair and right according to the law. In this case, the court will decide if these men broke the law by trying to make a child get married. Did you know? Sierra Leone is a beautiful country in West Africa, known for its lovely beaches and kind people. The children there are just as smart and curious as you are!

Why School is So Important

Every child deserves to go to school. School is where you learn to read and write. It's where you learn about numbers, science, and the world around you. School helps you make new friends and learn how to share and work together. It's where you discover your talents and what you love to do. When girls go to school, they learn to be strong and smart. They can grow up to be amazing leaders, doctors, teachers, or whatever they dream of. If a girl is forced to marry early, she usually has to stop going to school, and then all those dreams can disappear. We want every child to have a chance to chase their dreams! Did you know? When girls go to school, it helps their families and their whole country become stronger and better!

A Brighter Future for Children

This court case shows that people in Sierra Leone are serious about protecting their children. They want all boys and girls to stay in school and grow up safely. They want to make sure that child marriage stops so that every child can have a happy childhood and a bright future. It’s a big step forward! It tells everyone that children are special and need to be protected. It reminds grown-ups everywhere that it's their job to keep children safe and help them learn and grow.

What Can You Do?

Even though you are kids, you can help! If you ever see something that doesn't feel right for a child, or if someone you know is being treated unfairly, you can tell a trusted grown-up. Maybe it's your parent, a teacher, or another family member. Speaking up is very brave, and it can help make things better for everyone. Remember, your voice is powerful! Every child deserves to be happy, safe, and to go to school. This story from Sierra Leone is a reminder that we are all working together to make sure that every child in Africa has the chance to shine brightly and fulfill their biggest dreams! That's all for today from your kid-friendly news reporter. Keep learning, keep playing, and keep shining!
summary: In Sierra Leone, four men, including a father and husband, are in court because they tried to make a 17-year-old girl, who is still a child, get married, showing that countries are working hard to protect children and make sure they stay in school.
whyMatters: This story is super important because it shows that grown-ups are fighting to make sure all kids, especially girls, can go to school and stay safe, instead of being forced into grown-up things like marriage too early.

title: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
content: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

originalTitle: 'We could call it racist' - Ivory Coast boss sad at Schweinsteiger comments
title: Let's Play Fair and Be Kind! A Football Story
content:
Hello, my amazing friends all across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter here, ready to share a story about something we all love: football! We're talking about kicking the ball, scoring goals, and cheering for our favourite teams. But sometimes, even in fun games, grown-ups can say things that make others feel sad. Let’s talk about a time this happened with our friends from Ivory Coast.

A Game We All Love!

Football, or soccer, is a super fun game! Two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's net. Everyone runs, jumps, and tries their best. It makes us very happy when our team wins! But more than winning, football teaches us to play fair and be friends with everyone. Did you know that football is the most popular sport in the whole wide world? People play it in every country!

Meet the Elephants from Ivory Coast!

Our story today is about a wonderful country in Africa called Ivory Coast. Their football team is very famous and strong! They are even called the "Elephants" because they are so powerful and work together like a big family. The Elephants have many super talented players. They run fast, kick hard, and play with lots of energy! They train hard every day to be the best they can be. Did you know that Ivory Coast is in West Africa and has beautiful beaches? They also grow lots of cocoa beans, which are used to make yummy chocolate!

Words Can Hurt Feelings

Recently, the coach of the Ivory Coast team, a kind man named Emerse Fae, felt a little sad. Why? Because a famous football player from far away, from a country called Germany, said some words about the Elephants. This player, Mr. Bastian Schweinsteiger, said the Ivory Coast team played in a "wild" way. Now, playing with lots of energy is good! We love to see players run fast and be strong. But the word "wild" can sometimes sound unkind, especially when you are talking about people from a different place. It can make it seem like they are not playing smartly or fairly, even if they are! Did you know that sometimes, people say things without really thinking about how their words might make others feel? That's why it's important to always choose kind words.

What Does "Racist" Mean?

Coach Emerse Fae felt that what Mr. Schweinsteiger said might be a little bit like being "racist." This is a big word, but let's make it simple. Being racist means saying or doing unkind things to people just because of where they come from, or what color their skin is, or how they look. It’s like saying, "You are not as good as me because you are different." That’s not fair at all! Everyone is special and good in their own way. We all come from different places, but we are all human beings who deserve respect and kindness. We should never judge someone just because they are different. Did you know that treating everyone fairly is super important? It helps everyone feel happy and safe to be themselves!

Why Coach Emerse Fae Was Sad

When someone says unkind words about your team, your country, or your friends, it can make you feel sad or upset. Coach Emerse Fae felt sad because the words about his team felt unfair. He knows his team plays with skill and heart, not "wildly" in a bad way. He wanted everyone to respect his players and their amazing efforts. He knows how hard they work. Imagine if someone said something not nice about your best friend or your family. You would feel sad too, right? That's how Coach Fae felt. He wants people to celebrate the differences and strengths of all teams from all over the world. Did you know that feeling sad is okay? It tells us that something important has happened, and it helps us think about what is fair and right. Sharing your feelings helps too.

Being Kind and Playing Fair

In football, we always talk about "fair play." This means playing by the rules, being honest, and treating all players with respect, even those on the other team. It means shaking hands after the game, no matter who wins or loses. It’s not just about playing fair with our feet, but also with our words. When we talk about other teams or people, we should always be kind and respectful. We can say, "Wow, that team is so fast!" or "They have such strong players!" These are kind words that celebrate talent and hard work. Using good words makes the game much more fun for everyone involved. It shows that we value each other. Did you know that when you are kind to others, it makes you feel good too? Kindness is like a magic spell that makes everyone happy!

Let's Celebrate Everyone!

This story reminds us that football is a game for everyone. It brings people from all over the world together. We cheer, we laugh, and we learn from each other. Every team, every player, and every country has something special to share. Let’s always remember to use kind words and show respect to everyone. Whether they are from Ivory Coast, Germany, or right next door, everyone deserves to feel good about who they are and how they play. We can all be friends and celebrate the joy of football together! When we respect each other, the world becomes a brighter place. Did you know that different countries have different ways of playing football, and that's what makes the game even more exciting to watch? It's fun to see all the different styles! So, let's keep cheering for our favorite teams with big smiles and kind hearts! Let's remember that fair play and kind words make the game, and the world, a much better place for all of us. Go, Elephants! Go, every team! And go, all of you amazing kids!
summary: A coach from Ivory Coast felt sad because a player from Germany used words about his team that weren't kind, reminding us all to be respectful and fair in football and in life.
whyMatters: It's important because it teaches us that words are powerful, and we should always use kind words and respect everyone, no matter where they are from, especially in sports that bring people together.

content: President says his country will readmit genuine nationals but insists Europe must first verify deportees' identities.

originalTitle: Morocco jails 29, including politicians and sports figures, in drug trial
title: Big News from Morocco: Police Catch Bad Guys!
content:
Hello, super kids of Africa! Today, we have important news from a beautiful country called Morocco. Morocco is in Africa, just like many of our homes! Sometimes, grown-ups make big mistakes. And when they do, kind and brave people like the police and judges help make things right and keep everyone safe.

A Big Story in Morocco

Imagine a busy, bright city called Casablanca in Morocco. In this city, some grown-ups did things that were not good. They were involved with something called 'drugs'. Drugs are special things that make people sick and sad. They are against the rules and make our communities unsafe. So, it's very important that we always say NO to drugs and choose to be healthy and happy! Did you know Morocco is famous for its delicious oranges and beautiful historical cities?

What Happened?

A while ago, the police in Morocco started looking for people who were breaking these important rules about drugs. They worked very hard, like real-life superheroes, to find these people and keep everyone safe. They found 29 grown-ups who were doing bad things with drugs. That's a lot of people!

Who Were These People?

Guess what? Some of these people were grown-ups we call 'politicians'. Politicians are like the grown-ups who help run our towns and countries. They make rules and try to make life better for everyone. But these politicians made a big mistake by getting involved with drugs. Other people caught were 'sports figures'. These are like our favourite soccer players or athletes! They are usually heroes we look up to and admire. It was very sad that even these important people were caught doing something wrong. Did you know that many politicians and sports figures around Africa work very hard every day to make their countries better and to inspire young people like you to dream big and achieve great things?

What is a 'Trial'?

When grown-ups are caught breaking important rules, they go to a special place called a 'court'. In the court, there is a very wise person called a 'judge'. The judge listens to everyone carefully. They listen to what the police say and what the people who broke the rules say. This whole process, where everyone tells their story, is called a 'trial'. A trial helps to find out what really happened and if someone is guilty or not guilty.

The 'Escobar of the Sahara'

There was one very big leader of all these bad things. People gave him a special, scary nickname: the 'Escobar of the Sahara'. Sahara is a very, very big desert in Africa! So, this nickname meant he was a very important boss, who made many people do bad things with drugs. But the brave police were able to catch him, too! Did you know the Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert in the world? It's so big, it covers parts of many African countries, including Morocco! Imagine how vast and sandy it is.

The Judge's Decision: The 'Verdict'

After listening to everything very carefully and thinking deeply, the wise judge made a very important decision. This decision is called a 'verdict'. The judge said that the 29 people were indeed guilty of breaking the rules about drugs. For the most important people, like the 'Escobar of the Sahara' and some of the politicians, the judge said they would have to go to 'jail' for up to 12 years. Twelve years is a very, very long time!

What is 'Jail'?

Jail is a place where grown-ups go when they break very important rules and harm others. It's not a fun place at all. People go to jail to think about their mistakes and to learn to do better things when they come out. It also helps to keep our communities safe from people who might want to do more bad things. Imagine, 12 years is like from when you are a little baby until you are almost a big teenager!

Why This News is Important

This news is very important because it shows us that no matter who you are – even if you are a politician who helps run the country or a famous sports person – you must always follow the rules. Breaking rules, especially rules about bad drugs, makes everyone sad and unsafe. The police and judges in Morocco are working hard to make sure their country is a safe and happy place for all children and grown-ups. Did you know following rules helps us live together happily? It's like playing a game – everyone needs to follow the rules for it to be fair and fun for all the players!

Always Choose Good!

So, super kids, remember to always choose to do good things. Listen to your parents and teachers. Stay away from bad things like drugs. Help your friends and family. When everyone chooses to do good, our amazing African continent becomes an even brighter, safer, and more wonderful place for us all to live, learn, and grow! Hooray for good choices and safe communities!
summary: Police in Morocco caught 29 people, including some important grown-ups, who were doing bad things with drugs, and a judge decided they must go to jail to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that everyone must follow the rules to keep our communities safe and happy, and that people who do bad things will be caught.
